questions.question: 1. Function prototype is required in every program.

explanations.a: Incorrect, Function prototypes are not required in every program, but they are necessary when functions are defined after their usage or in separate files.
explanations.b: Correct. Function prototypes are not mandatory in every program, especially if the function is defined before it is used.

questions.question: 2. Functions can return more than one value at a time.

explanations.a: Incorrect, Standard C functions can only return a single value. Multiple values can be returned using pointers or structures, but not directly.
explanations.b: Correct. Functions in C return only one value directly.

questions.question: 3. What is the default return value of a function?

explanations.a: Correct. In C, if no return type is specified, the default is int.
explanations.b: Incorrect, 'Void' is not the default return type; it must be explicitly specified.
explanations.c: Incorrect, C does have a default return type, which is int.
explanations.d: Incorrect, Boolean is not a default return type in C.
